description: Extraction seams identified in Spring Music, aligned with ADR-001-decomposition.md. Grouped by business capability, not technical layer.
extraction_strategy: Strangler Fig with API Facade — extract one seam at a time, monolith stays live until contract tests pass
prerequisite: Characterization tests (The Pin) must be written against the current API before Phase 1 begins. No extraction without a safety net.

seams.name: Operational Safety
seams.description: Unauthenticated crash endpoints and wildcard Actuator exposure. Not a business capability — a security liability that must be removed before any deployment.
security_issues: GET /errors/kill calls System.exit(1) — kills the JVM, no auth required
GET /errors/fill-heap runs infinite memory allocation loop — causes OOM
application.yml exposes ALL Actuator endpoints publicly (env vars, heap dumps, full bean context)
seams.notes: Delete ErrorController entirely. Restrict Actuator to health and info only. This is Phase 0 — must happen before any extraction begins.

seams.name: Album Catalog Service
seams.description: The only real business capability: album CRUD. Controller, domain entity, and all repository implementations are grouped together because they must be extracted as a unit — a controller without its entity and repository is non-functional.
seams.notes: Album carries JPA annotations but is reused as-is for MongoDB — annotation conflict must be resolved during extraction. Target: Spring Boot 3.x, Java 17, PostgreSQL as default backend. CrudRepository interface provides a clean internal boundary. No circular dependencies to other capabilities.

seams.name: Data Initialization
seams.description: Hidden business rule: 'seed the catalog on first startup if empty'. Must travel with the Album Catalog Service extraction — if left behind, the extracted service starts empty with no demo data.
seams.notes: The count()==0 guard is an implicit business rule buried in an ApplicationReadyEvent listener — easy to miss. During extraction, make it explicit: migrate to a DataInitializer component or a Flyway seed migration inside the new service.

seams.name: Platform / Infrastructure Adapter
seams.description: CF service detection, profile mapping, and InfoController. This seam does not get extracted into a microservice — it dissolves naturally when the Cloud Foundry dependency is removed and replaced with standard SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE env vars.
seams.notes: SpringApplicationContextInitializer runs before the Spring context is built — untestable and brittle. InfoController does new CfEnv() directly (not injected — cannot be mocked). Short-term fix: inject CfEnv. Medium-term: replace CF profile detection with env-var-based config. This class dissolves, it is not extracted.
